level_signals: 4–6 years in revenue operations or related GTM role
Works in partnership with a senior operations leader who will mentor them
Base salary range $119K–$140K in New York

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 4–6 years in revenue operations, sales operations, business operations, or a related GTM strategy/analytics role, preferably within B2B SaaS.
Strong hands-on proficiency in Salesforce for reporting and analysis, and comfort working across CRM data and internal reporting/BI tools.
Demonstrated ability to maintain operational dashboards, scorecards, and recurring reporting packages with a high bar for data accuracy.

Curiosity and fluency around AI use cases for GTM and operations teams — experience or strong interest in leveraging AI GTM capabilities to automate recurring work, accelerate analysis, simplify how the field operates, and drive productivity gains.
Highly organized with strong attention to detail; able to manage multiple recurring cadences and ad hoc requests without dropping threads
Comfortable operating with ambiguity in a fast-scaling environment; proactive about surfacing gaps and risks early.
Strong communication and stakeholder skills; able to partner effectively across a field organization and present clean, decision-ready outputs to leadership.
An analytical mindset — able to move from a data pull to the "so what" and translate raw data into snapshots leaders can act on.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Preference for candidates based in New York or the surrounding area and able to commute to the office up to 3 days per week.
4–6 years in revenue operations, sales operations, business operations, or a related GTM strategy/analytics role, preferably within B2B SaaS.

explicitly_excluded: Current and future Visa sponsorship is not offered for this role

scope.surface: North America Value Engineering and Services organizations; embedded business partner role within Revenue Strategy & Operations
primary_metrics_inferred: Data accuracy in dashboards and scorecards
Operational cadence health (activity tracking, capacity reporting, pipeline hygiene)
Productivity gains from AI GTM automation
VE engagement intake and assignment efficiency
core_responsibilities: Run the operational rhythm: Support the day-to-day operating cadence of the Value Engineering and Services organizations - activity tracking, capacity reporting, and engagement pipeline hygiene across the field organization.
Own dashboards and scorecards: Maintain and update VE performance dashboards and operational scorecards on a weekly cadence, ensuring data accuracy across Salesforce and internal reporting tools.
Coordinate engagement intake and assignment: Assist in running VE engagement intake and assignment workflows and hiring coordination.
Prepare leadership reporting: Build the recurring reporting packages VE and Services leadership relies on, pulling structured data from CRM and the Celonis platform.
Support analytics on demand: Turn around ad hoc analytical requests from the VE Ops team.
Drive strategic initiatives: Partner on and help lead select strategic initiatives and planning workstreams for the VE and Services organizations.
Make the function better as you go: Spot friction in how the team operates and propose process improvements.

compensation.equity_form: Restricted Stock Units (RSUs) and merit-based refresh grants

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed ($119K–$140K base) in New York
RSUs and merit-based refresh grants confirmed from day one
Clear reporting structure — mentored by a senior ops leader
Generous parental leave policy (24 weeks primary, 12 weeks secondary) from day one
Hybrid model explicitly stated (up to 3 days/week)
red_flags: No visa sponsorship offered, current or future — strict constraint
Preference for New York/surrounding area with in-office commute expectation; not fully remote
Role is embedded with Value Engineering and Services, which may be niche; unclear growth path stated

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years range, comp, and mentorship framing)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description as enterprise SaaS B2B)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred from JD language 'base salary range below is for the role in New York')
ambiguities: The B2B SaaS preference ('preferably within B2B SaaS') appears in the years_of_experience must-have bullet, so it was extracted both as a qualifier on that hard requirement and as a separate soft nice-to-have — candidates without SaaS background are not explicitly excluded.

New York location is listed as a 'preference' rather than a hard requirement, but the role is described as 'Based in our New York hub', creating mild ambiguity about whether non-NY candidates would be considered. Treated as a soft nice-to-have.
